metadata.name: CDE Workflow
metadata.description: Information container states within the Common Data Environment defined in ISO 19650.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: A container of information managed within the Common Data Environment.

nodes.label: Work In Progress
nodes.description: State where information is being developed by its authoring team. Information can only be generated or amended in this state.

nodes.label: Shared
nodes.description: State where information is shared with other teams for coordination after being checked, reviewed, and approved.

nodes.label: Published
nodes.description: State where information has been authorized for use and is the most current revision.

nodes.label: Archived
nodes.description: State where superseded information is retained for audit and historical reference but is no longer approved for use.

nodes.label: Status Code
nodes.description: Property signifying the permitted use of the content of an information container, assigned based on workflow state.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Operational framework combining information production workflow and enabling technologies to facilitate collaborative production of information.

evidence.quote: Information containers start the information production workflow in the WORK IN PROGRESS state. This is the only state where information within an information container can be generated or amended by its appointed party.

When an information container is ready to be shared with the lead appointed party and other parties within the information production team, it transitions from the WORK IN PROGRESS state to the SHARED state.
Information containers that have been accepted by the appointing party, transition to the PUBLISHED state. This workflow state indicates that the information container is the most current revision.

Information containers that are superseded transition to the ARCHIVED state. This workflow state indicates that the information container has reached the end of the information production workflow, is no longer approved for its previously intended use.
Information containers may be archived without being published if they are superseded or no longer required before authorization.

In addition to a workflow state, each revision of an information container is assigned a status. The status of an information container is determined by which workflow state it is in, and the use for which the information within it has been approved.
All information production is undertaken via the common data environment.
evidence.source: ISO 19650-1:2026 Draft, Clause 3.2.10, Note 3 to entry

evidence.quote: The common data environment is an operational framework that combines the information production workflow and the enabling technologies.
The information production workflow has five workflow states... SHARED.
The information production workflow has five workflow states... PUBLISHED.
The information production workflow has five workflow states... ARCHIVED.
